commit.message:
feat(netrun-ui): add VS Code "Open Netrun Config" command

Add netrun-ui.openConfig command that finds netrun config files in the
workspace and opens the selected one in the flow editor. If only one file
exists it opens directly; if multiple, shows a QuickPick. Extracts shared
file-finding logic into pickNetrunFile helper reused by both openConfig
and openNode commands.

commit.message:
Fixes #2207: Add Run Now button for background workers on dashboard (#2307)

* Fixes #2207: Add Run Now button for background workers on dashboard

Add a trigger mechanism to BaseBackgroundLoop that allows interrupting
the sleep cycle for immediate execution, expose it through the
orchestrator, a new API endpoint, and a "Run Now" button in the
dashboard's SystemPanel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: fix trigger event race and add tests (PR #2307)

- _sleep_or_trigger now checks if the event is pre-set (trigger called
  during active work) and returns True immediately, so triggers fired
  during _execute_cycle are no longer silently dropped
- run() uses the returned bool instead of re-checking the event (the old
  check was always False since _sleep_or_trigger always cleared the event)
- Replace deprecated asyncio.ensure_future with asyncio.create_task
- Add TestBaseBackgroundLoopTrigger covering trigger/sleep_or_trigger

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: fix trigger event race and add tests (PR #2307)

- Fix run() bug: for run_on_startup=False workers, triggered disabled
  workers now execute work instead of silently continuing after sleep
- Fix _sleep_or_trigger: use except BaseException to properly cancel
  tasks on external CancelledError (asyncio.CancelledError is a
  BaseException in Python 3.8+)
- Add epic_sweeper_loop to _bg_loop_registry so it can be triggered
  from the dashboard
- Add regression test: trigger bypasses disabled check for
  run_on_startup=False workers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: T-rav-Hydra-Ops <t@koderex.dev>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Force Opus stereo encoding via Playwright addInitScript SDP patch

forcing stereo=1, sprop-stereo=1, cbr=1, maxaveragebitrate=256000
on Opus SDP fmtp lines. Handles both explicit and implicit
setLocalDescription flows.

commit.message:
Fix balance column always showing 0 for all players (#55)

Two root causes:

1. Missing Firestore composite indexes for collection-group queries.
   useAllFines/Payments/DuePayments use where('teamId','==',x) +
   orderBy('date'/'createdAt') — Firestore requires a composite index
   when filtering and ordering on different fields. Without these
   indexes queries fail silently in production, returning null data,
   which the page treats as []. Empty arrays → empty balance map → 0.

2. CSV-imported PAYMENT rows (Guthaben) had no category field set.
   usePlayerBalances only counts category-less payments when paid=true.
   If the CSV 'paid' column is empty, payments get paid=false and are
   silently skipped. Adding category=PAYMENT ensures they always count
   as credits, matching PaymentsService.createPayment() behaviour.

Closes #44

Co-authored-by: Heiko Rademacher <rademacher.heiko@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add opencode.json with external directory permission for tasks

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Instantly login: inject __session + navbarExp cookies to bypass reCAPTCHA

Instantly's login page uses Google reCAPTCHA which blocks headless Chrome.
Instead of trying to solve the captcha, inject the auth cookies directly:
- __session: the httpOnly session cookie used for API auth
- navbarExp: client-side cookie checked by isLoggedIn() function

The token is read from INSTANTLY_TOKEN env var.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CAS sharpening on Adreno GPU + Windows ML SR scaffold for NPU

CAS (Contrast Adaptive Sharpening) post-processing pass:
- New Sharpen.hlsl compute shader (AMD FidelityFX CAS algorithm)
- Runs on Adreno GPU after YCbCr→RGB decode each frame
- Selectively sharpens edges, preserves flat areas
- Adjustable 0–100 sharpness slider in toolbar (default 60)
- Dynamic constant buffer for per-frame sharpness updates

Windows.AI.MachineLearning pipeline (SrPipeline.cs):
- LearningModelDeviceKind.DirectXHighPerformance → Hexagon NPU on
  Snapdragon X / Copilot+ PCs
- Loads any ONNX SR/denoising model from Assets/sr_model.onnx
- Tile-friendly ImageFeatureValue binding
- Suitable models: ONNX Model Zoo super-resolution, DnCNN, Real-ESRGAN

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add chip flow hierarchy: agent promotion, referral linking, and union-level minting

  player_number assignment as referral codes, commission rate validation
  (40-70% in 5% steps), and pre-paid vs credit line configuration
- AgentService: Add linkPlayerByReferral() for player_number-based agent linking
- AgentService: Add assignPlayerToAgent() for bulk admin-level linking
- WalletService: Lock minting to Union owner when club is in a union;
  standalone clubs can still mint directly
- ChipFlowService: New service for complete hierarchy transfers
  (Union→Club→Agent→Player) with full wallet_transaction audit trail
- scripts/fund-chip-hierarchy.mjs: Execution script that promotes horses
  to agents, assigns players, and funds the complete chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent IDB hanging on duplicate boot — reuse connection or close first

When bootFamilyApp runs twice (cached session + manual login), the second
boot's initFamilyDatabase could hang because the first boot's IDB connection
was still open. Now:
- Reuses existing connection if same database (skip re-open)
- Closes old connection before opening new one
- Handles onblocked event to prevent infinite hang

commit.message:
fix: ensure ALL BusBars have relX1/relX2 after every AI edit response

Root cause: After AI edits, busbars lost relX1/relX2 properties needed
for full-width line rendering. Three failure modes:

1. BusBar created before the relX fix — passed through delta unchanged
   with no relX properties, frontend fell back to tiny SYMBOL_MAP symbol
2. AI returned modified busbar with relX at top-level (flat format)
   instead of inside properties — normaliseEl didn't recover them
3. AI's delta didn't modify the busbar, but the original element
   (from client) never had relX properties

Fix: Added ensureBusBarProps() post-processor that runs on ALL elements
before the response is sent, guaranteeing every BusBar/DoubleBusBar has
relX1/relY1/relX2/relY2 properties synced to element width. Also
recover top-level relX properties from flat AI delta responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pagination offset skip causing missing companies in demographic report

The offset was advancing by pageSize (10000) regardless of how many
records LinkedIn actually returned per page. If LinkedIn caps responses
below 10000, records in between were silently skipped. Now advances by
pageElements.length (actual records received) so no records are missed.

commit.message:
fix: exclude invalid timestamps in pending mode, surface auth errors

P1: Pending transactions (waiver/pending_trade) bypassed the 14-day
cutoff but also skipped invalid timestamp filtering, allowing rows with
timestamp <= 0 to leak through while the response claimed they were
excluded. Now invalid timestamps are always filtered first, then the
cutoff is conditionally applied.

P2: resolveUserTeamKey returned null for all non-OK responses (including
401/5xx), masking real auth-worker failures as TEAM_KEY_MISSING. Now
throws on non-OK responses so the error propagates with the actual
failure reason.

commit.message:
Add API key (crk_) authentication support to auth middleware

The auth middleware previously only accepted JWT tokens. The engine uses
crk_ API keys (created during device auth sign-in) to call the marketplace
sync endpoint. This adds API key validation by looking up the key in the
keys table, resolving the user and workspace, then proceeding with the
same auth context as JWT auth.
